query: Intel Foundry 14A Process Design Kit PDK release status lead customers intent-to-build test chips

mako.raw:
{"query":"Intel Foundry 14A Process Design Kit PDK release status lead customers intent-to-build test chips","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1540,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://newsroom.intel.com/intel-foundry/intel-foundry-gathers-customers-partners-outlines-priorities","title":"Intel Foundry Gathers Customers and Partners, Outlines Priorities - Intel Newsroom","content":"SAN JOSE, Calif., April 29, 2025 – Today at Intel Foundry Direct Connect, the company will share progress on multiple generations of its core process and advanced packaging technologies.
Tan will be joined on stage by ecosystem partners including Synopsys, Cadence, Siemens EDA and PDF Solutions to highlight collaboration in serving foundry customers.
Intel Foundry’s first production 16 nanometer (nm) tape-out is in the fab now, and the company is engaging with lead customers on a 12nm node and derivatives built in collaboration with UMC.
other risks and uncertainties described in this release, our 2024 Form 10-K, our Q1 2025 Form 10-Q, and our other filings with the SEC."},{"url":"https://semiwiki.com/forum/threads/intel-says-it-has-two-prospective-customers-for-14a-%E2%80%94-expects-to-hear-about-commitments-in-second-half-of-2026.24421/","title":"Intel says it has two prospective customers for 14A — expects to hear about commitments in second half of 2026 | SemiWiki","content":"Intel says it has two prospective customers for 14A — expects to hear about commitments in second half of 2026
The good news is that so far, the response from potential customers to its 14A process development kit (PDK) has been positive.
14A test chips at customers' hands, commitments in H2​
14A PDK 0.5 on track for Q1​"},{"url":"https://anysilicon.com/news/intel-begins-work-on-10a-and-7a-process-nodes-as-14a-roadmap-moves-toward-key-pdk-release/","title":"Intel Begins Work on 10A and 7A Process Nodes as 14A Roadmap Moves Toward Key PDK Release - AnySilicon","content":"May 26th, 2026 — Intel is already looking beyond its upcoming 18A and 14A process technologies, with early development reportedly underway for future 10A and 7A nodes.
Intel has reportedly already provided a 0.5 version of the 14A PDK to customers, with a more mature 0.9 PDK targeted for external customers in October 2026.
The reported 0.9 PDK release in October 2026 is therefore a critical step for customer adoption.
If 14A becomes the first major High-NA EUV node, then 10A and 7A would likely build on that manufacturing foundation.
For fabless companies, PDK maturity can determine how early a chip design team is willing to commit resources.
Intel’s early development work on 10A and 7A shows that the company is trying to build a long-term foundry roadmap beyond 18A and 14A.
However, the more immediate test is Intel 14A."},{"url":"https://intellionaire.substack.com/p/the-intellionaire-ep-24-unofficial","title":"The Intellionaire Ep. 24 - Unofficial Intel Foundry Direct Connect 2026","content":"So, without an official Foundry Direct Connect this year, I thought I would write up my own unofficial summary of Intel Foundry in 2026.
14A is Intel’s first true foundry-focused process node which involved consultation with prospective customers from the initial design stages.
Last year, Apple sold around 26 million Macs.
Considering that Fab 52 + 62 combined are expected to be producing up to 40,000 wafers per month, one could infer that Fab 52 alone, when fully tooled, will be doing 20,000 wafers per month.
Revenue for this from MediaTek I think may start to show on the books from as early as Q1 2026."},{"url":"https://www.eetimes.com/intel-foundry-we-are-listening-and-learning-from-our-customers/","title":"Intel Foundry: We Are Listening and Learning from Our Customers - EE Times","content":"Intel Foundry chief technology and operations officer Naga Chandrasekaran highlighted in his talk that Intel has invested almost $90 billion in capital over the last four years, with 20% of that in technology R&D investment and the remaining 80% in expanding its global footprint.
Intel Foundry said it has engaged with lead customers on the Intel 14A process technology, the successor to Intel 18A.
It has distributed an early version of the Intel 14A process design kit to lead customers, and it said multiple customers have expressed their intent to build test chips on the new process node."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Intel Foundry 14A Process Design Kit PDK release status lead customers intent-to-build test chips","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.thelec.net/news/articleView.html?idxno=10625","title":"Intel Accelerates 1.4nm Foundry Push, Targets Mass Production in 2029 < Semiconductor < 기사본문 - The Elec Inc.","content":"## Company to distribute 14A beta PDK to external customers in October as it races TSMC's A14 process. Intel plans to begin external distribution of the 0.9 process design kit (PDK) for its 14A process in October and aims to start mass production in 2029 after discussions with customers on production scale and product designs. Lip-Bu Tan, chief executive of Intel, said at the J.P. Morgan investor conference on May 19 (local time) that the company had already begun providing the 0.5 PDK for Intel Foundry's 14A process to customers and that development was progressing smoothly. A PDK is a comprehensive design toolkit that a foundry company provides to fabless semiconductor customers. The 0.9 PDK is a beta-stage version refined to more closely resemble actual mass-production conditions under a full 1.0 PDK. Tan said Intel is currently working with multiple customers on the 14A process and discussing what products will be built, which foundry fabs will manufacture them and what scale of production capacity will be required.","score":0.73299384,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://anysilicon.com/news/intel-begins-work-on-10a-and-7a-process-nodes-as-14a-roadmap-moves-toward-key-pdk-release","title":"Intel Begins Work on 10A and 7A Process Nodes as 14A Roadmap ...","content":"# Intel Begins Work on 10A and 7A Process Nodes as 14A Roadmap Moves Toward Key PDK Release. **May 26th, 2026 —** Intel is already looking beyond its upcoming 18A and 14A process technologies, with early development reportedly underway for future 10A and 7A nodes. The update signals that Intel Foundry wants to show customers a long-term advanced-node roadmap extending into the next decade, even as the company continues to work through the difficult transition from an internal manufacturing model to a competitive foundry business. According to recent industry reports, Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan confirmed that development activity has begun for 10A and 7A, while Intel 14A remains on track for a major process design kit milestone. Intel has reportedly already provided a 0.5 version of the 14A PDK to customers, with a more mature 0.9 PDK targeted for external customers in October 2026. Intel 14A is expected to be Intel’s first major process technology to use High-NA EUV lithography in volume manufacturing.","score":0.69746524,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.synopsys.com/blogs/chip-design/process-design-kits-intel-foundry.html","title":"Process Design Kits Accelerate Intel Foundry Adoption","content":"# Process Design Kits: How Synopsys and Intel Foundry Are Accelerating Customer Success. At the most advanced nodes, semiconductor innovation is driven less by individual breakthroughs and more by the efficient application of process technology. This is precisely where the Synopsys–Intel Foundry collaboration is most focused. As Intel Foundry pushes into the Angstrom era — marking a shift beyond nanometer‑class scaling toward near‑atomic dimensions — with process nodes such as Intel 18A and Intel 14A, the role of PDKs has expanded from basic enablement to full‑stack orchestration of design, analysis, and manufacturing readiness. “A PDK is essentially the building block for the foundry and for the process technology,” says Jonathan Knudsen, senior principal engineer at Intel Foundry. “It’s all of the EDA components and collateral that enable customers to take their ideas from concept to silicon and ultimately to high‑volume manufacturing.”. As one of Intel Foundry’s most distinctive innovations, PowerVia required new physical design, routing, and verification approaches to be embedded directly into PDK content and exposed coherently across the full design flow.","score":0.6369636,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://newsroom.intel.com/intel-foundry/intel-foundry-gathers-customers-partners-outlines-priorities","title":"Intel Foundry Gathers Customers and Partners, Outlines ...","content":"## [Naga Chandrasekaran, Intel Foundry chief technology and operations officer, displays a wafer made on the Intel 14A process node during a keynote at Intel Foundry Direct Connect on Tuesday, April 29, 2025, in San Jose, Calif. ## [Kevin O’Buckley (left), general manager of Foundry Services, speaks with Kevin Engel, chief operating officer of Amkor, during a keynote event at Intel Foundry Direct Connect on Tuesday, April 29, 2025, in San Jose, Calif. ## [Kevin O’Buckley, general manager of Foundry Services, displays a next-generation test chip demonstrating Intel Foundry’s advanced packaging capabilities during a keynote at Intel Foundry Direct Connect on Tuesday, April 29, 2025, in San Jose, Calif. SAN JOSE, Calif., April 29, 2025 – Today at Intel Foundry Direct Connect, the company will share progress on multiple generations of its core process and advanced packaging technologies.","score":0.56460327,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/wccftech/posts/a-wild-rumor-suggests-that-intel-has-succeeded-in-roping-in-another-client-for-i/1459702452823342","title":"A Wild Rumor Suggests Intel Has Roped In MediaTek's Dimensity","content":"Intel's 14A is expected to be production-ready in 2027, with early versions of process design kit (PDK) coming to external customers early this","score":0.5401241,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.81,"request_id":"1332e641-f206-4ce0-98c3-bebba30678c0"}
